notes: Top-tier PRS / NRL Hunter chassis with full-length monolithic Picatinny forend, integrated ARCA dovetail bottom rail, and the M-LOK + ARCA-equipped weight system. Buyers pick this when they want one chassis that does competitive PRS, ELR, and tactical match work without compromise.

Step down from the ACC Elite that keeps the ARCA forend and weighted barricade-stop system but ships without the upgraded folder and skeletonized buttstock. The chassis serious club-level PRS shooters buy when the Elite's price tag is too rich.

Hunter-weight crossover chassis with M-LOK forend slots and a folding adjustable stock, sized for backcountry hunters who still want chassis-level fit and accessory mounting. Popular NRL Hunter pick because it lands well under the discipline's weight ceiling.

Solid one-piece aluminum chassis aimed at the budget-conscious match shooter, pairing an M-LOK forend with a fixed buttstock that takes MDT skeleton add-ons. Used as a starter platform for shooters building their first dedicated bolt-gun match rifle.

Modular two-piece chassis built around an AR-style buffer-tube interface so users can run any AR pistol grip and aftermarket buttstock. The cheapest path into the MDT ecosystem and a common upgrade for a factory Savage or Tikka hunting rifle.

Drop-in V-block chassis with a free-float M-LOK forend that bolts under a factory barreled action with no gunsmithing. The de facto upgrade for shooters who want to convert a hunting rifle to a chassis without buying a new action.

Polymer-and-aluminum chassis with a vertical pistol grip, adjustable cheek riser, and AICS-pattern magazine well — the unofficial benchmark sub-$500 chassis. New PRS shooters and tactical-class hunters buy this as their first upgrade off a factory stock.

KRG's flagship modular chassis, with the W3 folding adjustable buttstock, Spigot Mount forend tubes, and full inlet for AICS magazines. Long-time staple in PRS Tactical class and military training programs.

Hunter-targeted chassis that keeps the Bravo's polymer feel but adds an M-LOK aluminum forend and adjustable folder to hit a backcountry-friendly weight. A common pick for NRL Hunter and Western big-game rigs.

Long-running aluminum chassis that put MPA on the PRS map, machined from 6061 with a vibration-dampening core and a deep barricade-friendly forend. The reference 'aluminum brick' chassis a generation of top PRS finishers ran.

MPA's modular flagship: composite-walled chassis with a solid-aluminum core, ARCA forend, and the company's Enhanced Vertical Grip. Top PRS Open shooters use this as the no-compromise platform when weight rules don't bite.

Lighter-weight MPA chassis that keeps the Matrix-style ARCA forend and adjustable folder while shedding mass for hunters and run-and-gun NRL Hunter shooters. Sized for a 22 to 24-inch barrel without nosing into PRS Open weights.

Hand-laid fiberglass chassis stock with an aluminum bedding block and integral barricade stop, the dominant 'wood-feel' platform in PRS Open at the moment. Picked by shooters who want a stock-feel chassis with the rigidity of a true skeleton.

Hunter-weight version of the Foundation aesthetic, with a slimmer forend and lighter buttstock layup so it can build into a sub-10-pound complete rig. NRL Hunter and Western mountain shooters choose this when they want Foundation feel under weight rules.

Modular aluminum chassis with a tool-less adjustable buttstock and M-LOK forend, the workhorse XLR offering for club PRS, NRL Hunter, and dedicated trainers. Common upgrade once a shooter outgrows the entry-tier chassis.
XLR's competition-tier chassis with a wide ARCA forend, integrated bag-rider buttstock, and adjustable balance weights. Built for shooters running a heavy match rifle in PRS Open.

Hand-laid carbon-fiber-shelled hunting stock with a mini-chassis-like aluminum bedding block, sub-3-pound bare weight. The benchmark hunter stock for shooters building a backcountry custom rifle who don't want a full chassis.

Carbon-shelled PRS-pattern chassis stock with an integral aluminum mini-chassis, vertical grip, adjustable cheek and LOP, and barricade-friendly forend. PRS Open shooters who like a stock feel pick this over a pure-aluminum chassis.

Fiberglass-reinforced PRS-style stock with an aluminum bedding pillar, vertical grip, and adjustable cheek piece. The de facto sub-$500 PRS-pattern stock for shooters who want a competition-shape stock without a chassis budget.

Hunter-weight Grayboe in the same fiberglass-reinforced layup as the Ridgeback, sized for a backcountry hunting rifle. Common upgrade for a Tikka T3x or Howa 1500 hunter that needs better ergonomics than the factory stock.
Skeletonized aluminum chassis built by a benchrest-pedigreed shop, with an ARCA forend and a folding buttstock. Picked by accuracy-first PRS Open shooters and serious benchrest crossovers.

Premium Canadian-machined aluminum chassis with full Picatinny / M-LOK forend, integrated ARCA, and Cadex's adjustable folding stock. Used by precision-rifle shooters who want a near-defense-grade chassis without the export-controlled programmatic kit.

Lighter-weight Cadex chassis aimed at hunters and tactical shooters who want Cadex fit and finish under a backcountry weight budget. Clearly hunter-shape forend with M-LOK slots and an adjustable folder.

Swedish-made Spuhr Ideal Chassis System with a one-piece machined aluminum body and integrated Picatinny rails along the forend. ELR and tactical-match shooters who want Spuhr's mounting precision build on this.

Rem 700-footprint chassis from the JP precision-rifle ecosystem, built around an AR-style lower interface so any AR pistol grip and buffer-tube stock fits. Picked by shooters cross-pollinating AR ergonomics into a bolt gun.

Long-running MDT chassis with an AR pistol-grip and buffer-tube interface, full free-float forend, and AICS magazine well. Still the value pick for shooters wanting an AR-ergonomic bolt gun on a budget — kept in the lineup alongside the newer ESS for the AR15-buttstock crowd.
